Cosine similarity measures the angle between two vectors rather than their Euclidean distance. For normalized vectors (unit norm), cosine similarity equals the dot product, making it computationally cheap. Sentence embedding models typically output L2-normalized vectors, so cosine similarity and dot product are interchangeable in that regime. A value of 1.0 means the vectors are identical in direction; 0.0 means orthogonal; −1.0 means diametrically opposed.
